setting.note: The book calls the city Kristiania (Christiania in its own spelling); that is the old name for Oslo, which the capital carried until 1925. The unnamed narrator paces it in a fever of starvation, up and down Carl Johann Street, out to the fortress by the sea, and down to the wharves. Hamsun keeps to real streets a hungry man can still walk, and it brings Norway into the atlas.

rights.originalWork: Public domain. Knut Hamsun, first published 1890; the author died in 1952.
rights.translation: English translation by George Egerton (1899); she died in 1945, so the translation is public domain in the US and UK/EU.
rights.territoryNotes: Public domain in the US and UK/EU: Hamsun died 1952, Egerton 1945.
rights.attribution: Knut Hamsun, Hunger (1890), trans. George Egerton. Text from Project Gutenberg.

palette.note: The green blanket Hans Pauli lent him, the last thing he owns and can neither keep warm under nor bear to pawn. A dark green that clears WCAG AA on paper.
